LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSED THAT STEVEN WERTH, ONE OF THE ATTORNEYS FOR DEFENDANT E.A.H., COACHED HIS SON'S LITTLE LEAGUER TEAM LAST YEAR AND THAT JUDGE KAHN, WHILE HE WAS AN ASSOCIATE WITH HELLER, EHRMAN, WHITE & MCAULIFFE IN 1983-87, REPRESENTED THE ECUMENICAL ASSOCIATION FOR HOUSING, BUT THAT NEITHER OF THESE FACTS AFFECT HIS ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THERE WAS NO OBJECTION BY EITHER PARTY. DEFENDANTS JOHN HUNTER AND EAH, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO MARIN COUNTY IS DENIED. VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO PURSUANT TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 12965(B) AND THE PORTION OF THE MOTION BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES IS PREMATURE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County
Off Calendar May-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pamela S. Helman, Esq., of Low Ball & Lynch, Tel. 415.981.6630, appearing for Defendants and moving party.
Ghazaleh Modarresi, Esq., of the Armstrong Law Firm, Tel. 415.928.1293, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

Judge Kahn disclosed that Steven Werth, one of the attorneys for defendant E.A.H., coached his son's little leaguer team last year and that Judge Kahn, while he was an associate with Heller, Ehrman, White & McAuliffe in 1983-87, represented the Ecumenical Association for Housing, but that neither of these facts affect his ability to be fair and impartial in this case. There was no objection by either party.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOHN HUNTER and EAH, INC.'s Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County is Denied. Venue is proper in San Francisco pursuant to Government Code section 12965(b) and the portion of the motion based on convenience of the witnesses is premature. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
